question: Will the Saints win the NFC Championship?

evidences.title: New Orleans Saints odds 2025: NFC South division winner
evidences.content: NFC South Winner 2025-26: These odds make some sense as the Bucs have been the most consistent team over the past few seasons, finishing 10-7 last season, to win the NFCS for the 4th consecutive year. The Falcons, who finished 8-9 last season will look to improve with Michael Penix at the helm and the NFL’s most expensive backup, Kirk Cousins on the bench, for now. However, I do disagree with the Panthers and Saints odds. They both finished with a 5-12 record last season but the Saints went through a lot during those 17 weeks, including a plague of injuries and the firing of their head coach. I think the Saints have the stronger team when healthy but given that their starting quarterback is still unknown at the moment, I can understand the odds placement. evidences.title: 2025-26 NFC Championship Odds - NFC Conference Champions
evidences.content: The Philadelphia Eagles and the Washington Commanders are the last teams standing and will face off for the NFC title and a trip to Super bowl 59. The defending conference champion San Francisco 49ers did not reach the playoffs, so we know there will be a new representative from the NFC in the Super Bowl. There were more dark horses in the NFC Championship odds at NFL sportsbooks that were in play this season than in the AFC, where underdogs needed to get through the likes of Patrick Mahomes and the Chiefs, Josh Allen and the Bills or Lamar Jackson and the Ravens. [...] They also knocked off playoff contenders, like the Rams and Packers (twice), Ravens, Steelers and Commanders. Their losses were to NFC South champ Tampa Bay, Atlanta and Washington. The Eagles play hard-nosed football on offense and defense. They can beat you on the ground or they can beat you via the passing game, plus they play solid special teams. [...] ## Longshot Odds to Win the NFC Championship There are two candidates remaining to dethrone the 49ers when assessing the NFC Championship odds. The Eagles and Commanders are the last teams standing in the list of favorites in NFC Championship odds, according to...

evidences.title: PFSN gives Saints second-best odds to go last-to-first in NFC South
evidences.content: This is shown as well by the PFSN NFL Playoff Predictor, which analyzes data and produces an outcome for each NFL matchup for the 2026 season, all the way through to the Super Bowl. Recently, they put out a graphic indicating which teams were most likely to go last-to-first in their respective division based on this model. At the top is the Detroit Lions, with 31.2% of their models having them take the division back. Next up, though, was New Orleans, sitting at a respectable 23.0%. [...] 23 percent seems a tad low given how much the team has improved, but the Carolina Panthers look mighty good after a few strong additions, and the Saints still have gaps to fix. In the one simulation this writer ran, the Saints won the division at 8-9, which seems rather fitting given the state of the NFC South, and then went on to take on the 12-5 Seattle Seahawks in the first round of the playoffs. You can imagine how that went. It is good to see predictive models favoring New Orleans a bit, though, after two really difficult seasons back to back, things are looking up for the Black and Gold in 2026. This article originally appeared on Saints Wire: PFSN has Saints and Lions as most likely to go...

They scored 19.9 points per game (24th in the league) while their defense conceded 23.4 (19th). The Saints host the Arizona Cardinals in Week 1, with kickoff at 1 p.m. ET on Sunday, Sept. 7. evidences.title: 2025-26 NFL Playoff Bracket - Super Bowl Matchup & ...
evidences.content: As we enter Week 18, there are still two playoff spots up for grabs, one from each conference. The Ravens and Steelers will play on SNF for the AFC North title. With Pittsburgh currently being one game up on Baltimore, a tie would result in the Steelers winning the division. We see a similar scenario in the NFC South, where the Buccaneers and Panthers will play on Saturday for the division title. However, Carolina could still win the NFC South with a loss. If the Falcons beat the Saints in Week 18, and thre Bucs beat the Panthers, the three teams would be tied at 8-9. The Panthers own the tiebreaker between the three teams. So, the Bucs not only need to win, but they also need the Saints to beat the Falcons in Week 18. [...] Updated 2026 NFL Playoff Bracket ahead of Super Bowl 60 The 2025-26 NFL regular season kicked off on Thursday, September 4, 2025. The regular season will conclude after all Week 18 games are played on January 4, 2026. The 2026 NFL playoffs are scheduled to begin January 10 with Super Wildcard Weekend. The 2026 Super Bowl will be played Sunday, February 8, 2026. The image above – which will be updated after each game of the 2025-26 regular season – displays t...

evidences.title: New Orleans Saints schedule 2025: Takeaways, predictions - ESPN
evidences.content: ## ESPN # New Orleans Saints schedule 2025: Takeaways, predictions Copy Link METAIRIE, La. -- The New Orleans Saints' 2025 schedule was released along with the rest of the NFL slate Wednesday. The Saints will be able to ease into the schedule with a young quarterback by starting their first two games at home. But things quickly ramp up from there with trips to Seattle and Buffalo. The most important bulk of their schedule comes in the back half of the season, mostly after the Week 11 bye. The Saints don't play a division opponent until hosting the Buccaneers in Week 8 and have five divisional matchups in the final nine weeks. That could give first-year coach Kellen Moore and his team some time to get acclimated before tackling the majority of the NFC South slate. [...] The Saints are going into this season with low expectations outside the building -- they don't have a prime-time game scheduled for the first time in a quarter century. If they have success this season, they'll do it by flying under the national radar. Here's what's in store for the Saints: ## Saints 2025 regular-season schedule Week 1: Sept. 7 vs. Arizona Cardinals Week 2: Sept. 14 vs. San Francisco 49ers Week 3:...

evidences.content: PFSN PFSN Las Vegas Raiders quarterback Geno Smith (7) talks with head coach Pete Carroll and quarterbacks coach Greg Olsen in a game against the Cleveland Browns during the fourth quarter at Allegiant Stadium. # NFL Playoff Elimination Scenarios: Saints, Titans, Raiders Out, Vikings On the Bubble With a busy Week 13 schedule, here are the teams who are facing or have already been eliminated from the playoff race for the 2025 NFL season. With Week 13 in the NFL nearing its conclusion, we are starting to reach a point in the season where some pictures are starting to take clear shape across the league. While some teams have solidified themselves in the playoff race, others have fallen by the wayside. [...] For the Jets, a victory over the Atlanta Falcons that involved a spectacular comeback in the fourth quarter, including 10 points in the final two minutes, was enough to stave off elimination for one more week. However, the Saints and the Titans weren’t so lucky. Despite multiple opportunities, Tyler Shough and New Orleans failed to capitalize against the Dolphins, losing 21-17 and officially ending any playoff hopes the organization might have had. For Tennessee, there was no s...
